violations.text: Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was supplied at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, whole cooked potatoes (83 degrees F) were left at room temperature in a large stock pot after cooking approximately 2 hours before inspection. Potatoes were relocated to a baking sheet and placed in the walk-in freezer at time of inspection for rapid cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Boxes of produce and containers of various food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Firm to obtain additional food storage racks to accommodate volume of food storage at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink in the bakery. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023

Handwashing signage is missing at the hand sinks in the kitchen and bakery. A sign or poster that reminds employees to wash their hands shall be supplied at each designated hand sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Items in the mobile walk-in unit were observed held at 49-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed of most time-temperature foods and moved other products that were delivered today. The PIC contacted the mobile walk-in rental company for repair/replacement. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023

A flat of eggs was observed held at 75 degrees Fahrenheit in the kitchen. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023
The lids of the flour bins located in the bakery area were not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
Inspector observed that the handsink in the kitchen did not supply the user with adequate water pressure for handwashing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit and freezer unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. A new walk-in is scheduled to be constructed and completed within weeks. In the meantime, no product is to be stored below the condensation leak. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023
